title: down entity
text: down entity In a component structure, an inner entity that represents the many side in a one-to-many relationship with the outer entity. So called because one usually travels down a model diagram to go from the one entity to the many entity. By default, Uniface supports full create, read, update, and delete operations on both entities. Related Links entity A development object that defines a logical data aggregation and its associated default characteristics and behavior. It may be mapped to a table in a database, but it can also represent transient data, a collection of controls, or constant data. one-to-many relationship A relationship between two entities (for example, X and Y), where more than one occurrence of Y can exist for each occurrence of X, and only one occurrence of X exists for all related occurrences of Y. up entity In a component structure, an inner entity that represents the one side in a one-to-many relationship with the outer entity. Deleting the outer entity is subject to delete constraints. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
